statement.p:
Identify which of the items are quadratic equations.
The equation 2x^2+5x=7
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The equation 5-2x=3
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The equation 15-x^3=3x^2+9x
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The equation (x+3)(x-4)=0
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The equation x(x+1)(x-1)=0
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The expression x^2-5x+6
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.
The equation (2x-3)(x+5)=12
?
is
is not a quadratic equation.

li.p: The equation 2x^2+5x=7 is a quadratic equation. To write it in standard form, simply subtract 7 from both sides.
The equation 5-2x=3 is not quadratic. It is a linear equation.
The equation 15+x^3=3x^2+9x is not a quadratic equation because of the x^3 term.
The equation (x+3)(x-4)=0 is a quadratic equation. If we expand the left-hand side of the equation, we would get something in standard form.
The equation x(x+1)(x-1)=0 is not a quadratic equation. If we expanded the left-hand side of the equation, we would have an expression with an x^3 term, which automatically makes it not quadratic.
The expression x^2-5x+6 is not a quadratic equation; its not an equation at all. Instead, this is a quadratic expression.
The equation (2x-3)(x+5)=12 is a quadratic equation. Multiplying out the left-hand side, and subtracting 12 form both sides, we would have a quadratic equation in standard form.

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.1.2) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "parserPopUp.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context('Numeric'); $yes=PopUp(['?','is', 'is not'],1); $no=PopUp(['?','is', 'is not'],2);
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Identify which of the items are quadratic equations. a. The equation [`2x^2+5x=7`] [_]{$yes}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The equation [`5-2x=3`] [_]{$no}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The equation [`15-x^3=3x^2+9x`] [_]{$no}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The equation [`(x+3)(x-4)=0`] [_]{$yes}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The equation [`x(x+1)(x-1)=0`] [_]{$no}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The expression [`x^2-5x+6`] [_]{$no}{5} a quadratic equation.
a. The equation [`(2x-3)(x+5)=12`] [_]{$yes}{5} a quadratic equation.
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION a. The equation [`2x^2+5x=7`] _is_ a quadratic equation. To write it in standard form, simply subtract [`7`] from both sides.
a. The equation [`5-2x=3`] _is not_ quadratic. It is a linear equation.
a. The equation [`15+x^3=3x^2+9x`] _is not_ a quadratic equation because of the [`x^3`] term.
a. The equation [`(x+3)(x-4)=0`] _is_ a quadratic equation. If we expand the left-hand side of the equation, we would get something in standard form.
a. The equation [`x(x+1)(x-1)=0`] _is not_ a quadratic equation. If we expanded the left-hand side of the equation, we would have an expression with an [`x^3`] term, which automatically makes it not quadratic.
a. The expression [`x^2-5x+6`] _is not_ a quadratic equation; it's not an _equation_ at all. Instead, this is a quadratic _expression_.
a. The equation [`(2x-3)(x+5)=12`] _is_ a quadratic equation. Multiplying out the left-hand side, and subtracting [`12`] form both sides, we would have a quadratic equation in standard form.
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
